name: bijou-i18n Tools
designPattern: Dedicated Localization Workflow and Catalog Tooling Package
requirements: Tests ARE the Spec roadmap principle

userStory.asA: builder or localization maintainer working on a Bijou application
userStory.iWant: a dedicated tooling package for authoring catalogs, spreadsheets, and translation workflow tasks
userStory.soThat: real localization work can happen through source-hash tracking, export/import, validation, and compilation without polluting the runtime package

acceptanceCriteria: Bijou defines a dedicated tooling package boundary separate from the runtime i18n package
The tooling package supports authoring catalogs that can be compiled into runtime catalogs
The tooling package marks translations stale automatically when source-locale content changes
The tooling package supports spreadsheet-friendly export and import workflows for missing or stale translations
The tooling package supports references across catalogs or shared data rows as an authoring concern
The tooling package can validate and normalize references before runtime consumption
The tooling package has a home for pseudo-localization and long-string or RTL stress tooling
The tooling package does not require any specific spreadsheet vendor or remote service by default
backwardCompatibility: This is additive and primarily build-time. Existing apps can continue shipping in-memory English defaults until they opt into authoring catalogs and tooling workflows.

inScope: Compilation from authoring catalogs to runtime catalogs
outScope: Owning shell/component wrapping or marquee rendering policies
A full persistence layer for application settings

testPlan.goldenPath: Write failing tests for stale detection, selective export of missing/stale entries, import merge behavior, and reference compilation into runtime-safe catalogs. Then prove the generated runtime catalogs can be loaded by the runtime i18n package without extra transformation.
edgeCases: A source string changes and only affected translations become stale
A spreadsheet import updates one locale while leaving others untouched
A referenced glossary or data entry is missing
A cyclic reference is reported before runtime
Pseudo-localization reveals overflow risk in compact shell copy
testPlan.fuzzStress: Tabular import/export parsing and reference graphs may warrant fuzzing later, especially once spreadsheet connectors or CSV edge cases grow. v0 should focus on deterministic normalization and explicit validation failures.
failureCases: Tooling logic leaks into the runtime package
The workflow assumes a specific spreadsheet provider instead of a generic tabular import/export seam
Source-string changes do not automatically mark translations stale
Reference-heavy catalogs can only fail at runtime instead of during compilation
Pseudo-localization and overflow stress stay as ad hoc scripts instead of having an official tooling home

estimatedComplexity.linesOfCode: ~300-700 lines for authoring-model types, hashing, export/import, normalization, and focused tests
estimatedComplexity.timeComplexity: Compilation is approximately O(E + R) over catalog entries E and validated references R, excluding external spreadsheet IO
